description: Crab cakes are patties made primarily from crab meat held together with a small amount of binder and seasonings, then pan-fried, baked, or broiled. They are especially associated with the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States.
core_ingredients: Binder such as breadcrumbs or cracker crumbs
preparation_steps: Mix crab meat gently with binder and seasonings without breaking it up too much.
Chill if needed to help them hold shape.
Cook until golden and heated through.

health_advice.benefits: Good source of seafood protein and can be relatively light when not heavily fried.
health_advice.risks: May be high in sodium; lower-quality versions can contain more filler than crab.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose versions with a high proportion of crab meat and lighter cooking methods.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional crab cakes are seafood-based. Gluten-free versions are possible with gluten-free crumbs or other binders.

serving_info.typical_serving: 2 to 3 small cakes or 1 large cake
